Chainlist is a list of EVM networks. Users can use the information to connect their wallets and Web3 middleware providers to the appropriate Chain ID and Network ID to connect to the correct chain.

ChainList

Add Your Network Add Your RPC

Chainlist: Your Gateway to Seamless Web3 Connectivity

Discover how Chainlist helps users connect to EVM-compatible networks effortlessly with a curated list of reliable RPC endpoints.

Why Use Chainlist for EVM Networks?

Chainlist simplifies blockchain interactions by providing verified RPC endpoints, ensuring seamless wallet and dApp integration.

How to Connect Your Wallet with Chainlist

Follow a step-by-step guide on using Chainlist to find and add RPC networks directly to your Web3 wallet.

Chainlist and Web3 Middleware: Enhancing Compatibility

Learn how Chainlist bridges the gap between wallets and middleware providers, improving the performance and efficiency of Web3 applications.

Future of Chainlist: Expanding the RPC Network Database

Stay updated on Chainlist’s ongoing expansion, ensuring support for new EVM networks and maintaining up-to-date RPC connections.

What is Chainlist?

Understand the core purpose of Chainlist and how it serves as a crucial tool for seamless blockchain connectivity.

How Secure is Chainlist?

Explore the security measures Chainlist implements to verify and provide trusted RPC endpoints for EVM networks.

Adding Custom RPCs with Chainlist

Find out how to manually add and configure custom RPC endpoints to your Web3 wallet using Chainlist.

Supported Networks on Chainlist

Discover the various EVM-compatible networks that Chainlist supports and how users can easily switch between them.

Integrating Chainlist with Web3 Applications

Learn how developers can leverage Chainlist to enhance the connectivity and functionality of their Web3 applications.
